cycles.name: Phase 0 — Spine
cycles.description: The hard-prerequisite foundation cycle: scaffold + Python env, the 8 spine components (feature_list.schema.json, evidence_collector, spec_validator/Z3, the 6 hooks: Stop/PreToolUse/PostToolUse-stub/SubagentStop/SessionStart/PreCompact wiring, the 4 agent definitions initializer/implementer/verifier/research, worktree_manager, feature_list_init), and the Phase-0 CI gates (formal-verification, coverage-gate stub, Playwright). Every spine component must be coded, wired and smoke-tested (tasks 16/17) before any Phase-1 work item leaves Backlog. Gate: zero unproven in-scope items + green smoke/e2e.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md '### Phase 0 — Spine' (tasks 1-17). Carries the 31 stories labelled phase:0 across E1(8)/E2(3)/E3(5)/E4(2)/E5(1)/E6(3)/E7(1)/E8(7). Set as the single ACTIVE cycle at provisioning time (start_date = today, contiguous non-overlapping with Phase 1).

cycles.name: Phase 1 — Verification Depth
cycles.description: Adds real verification teeth on top of the spine: post_tool_use_hook (lint+SAST+wiring feedback, non-blocking exit 1), wiring_checker (AST dead-code → WIRING coverage items), orphan_detector (bidirectional traceability), the full coverage_query.rego zero-evidence merge policy (replacing the Phase-0 stub), and the 5-layer verifier depth (structural/semantic/behavioral/security). Carryover of any unfinished Phase-0 item is transferred in via the transfer-issues API before this cycle goes active.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md '### Phase 1 — Verification Depth' (tasks 18-21+). Carries the stories labelled phase:1 (E4 ×2, E5 ×2, E7 ×1). Upcoming at provisioning; becomes active when Phase 0 closes.

cycles.name: Phase 2 — Durable State
cycles.description: Stands up the 8-table Postgres durable store (requirements, coverage_items, traceability_links, evidence_records, run_state, domain_baseline_checklists, requirement_versions, gate_audit_log; migrations 001-008), evidence store_to_postgres (Property 19 full round-trip), resumed-state integrity (state_integrity.py + SessionStart extension writing run_state.resume_integrity_ok), and the Postgres-unavailable file-backed fallback contract (Note N-25). This is the cycle that makes resumed state real and the fail-closed availability contract enforceable.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md '### Phase 2 — Durable State'. Carries the stories labelled phase:2 (E3 ×1, E5 ×3, E6 ×1, E7 ×5). Sequential after Phase 1.

cycles.name: Phase 3 — Observability
cycles.description: Makes the control plane legible: OTel gate-decision telemetry on every PreToolUse allow/block (event, tool, decision, reason, requirement_id), the immutable gate_audit_log + audit-chain-verify CI gate, traceability_writer (commit-has-req-id gate + traceability-gate CI), the epistemic-integrity / omission-declaration gates (REQ-SPEC-016/017/018) that ungate Research, and the Plane↔Agent bidirectional sync surfaces. This cycle lights up the dashboards and the Command Center.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md '### Phase 3 — Observability'. Carries the stories labelled phase:3 (E5 ×1, E6 ×2, E7 ×3, E8 ×3). Sequential after Phase 2.

cycles.name: Phase 4 — Property-Based Tests (Full Suite)
cycles.description: Hardening cycle: lands the full Hypothesis property-test suite (all 32 properties — schema invariant, four-field evidence gate, identity-mutation blockade, stop-hook unproven-blocks, no-progress/cap→HANDOFF, scope/plan gates, orphan detection, OPA zero-evidence-at-merge, evidence round-trip, etc.) plus the deepeval-gate and zap-baseline merge gates, aligning every property assertion with the reconciled status-edge and in_scope semantics.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md '### Phase 4 — Property-Based Tests (Full Suite)'. Carries the stories labelled phase:4 (E6 ×1) plus the property-test subtasks distributed across all epics. Sequential after Phase 3; closing this cycle = required (0-4) platform GA.

cycles.name: Phase 5 — Durable Orchestration (optional)
cycles.description: OPTIONAL per the tasks.md overview (Phase 5 = optional Temporal/Inngest durable execution). Replaces the in-process loop with a durable workflow engine so multi-day runs survive process death, with the same gate semantics enforced at workflow-step boundaries. Kept as a distinct upcoming cycle so it never blocks the required 0-4 GA; activate only if the durable-execution track is greenlit.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md Overview: 'Phase 5 = optional Temporal/Inngest durable execution'. No phase:5 label exists yet — provision the cycle empty and assign items via cycle-issues when the track opens. Sequential, upcoming, low priority.

cycles.name: Phase 6 — Predictive Routing (optional)
cycles.description: OPTIONAL per the tasks.md overview (Phase 6 = optional predictive routing). Adds the prediction layer that pre-routes slices to the right agent/verifier path — with the hard independence invariant that prediction variables can NEVER influence gate decisions (spec_validator independence check). Final upcoming cycle; gated behind Phase 5 and behind a passing independence proof.
cycles.maps_to: .kiro tasks.md Overview: 'Phase 6 = optional predictive routing'. No phase:6 label yet — provision empty, assign via cycle-issues when opened. Sequential, upcoming, lowest priority. Independence invariant is the activation gate.

modules.name: E1 — Core Plane Infrastructure & Self-Hosting
modules.description: The self-hosted Plane substrate: the 10 application services (web/space/admin/live/api/worker/beat-worker/migrator) + 4 backing stores (plane-db Postgres 15.7, plane-redis Valkey 7.2, plane-mq RabbitMQ 3.13, plane-minio MinIO) + Caddy proxy on host port 8090. Owns durability (named volumes, restart_policy), backup/restore, migrations, scaling, and health/readiness so the REST+Webhook API is a health-verified reachable endpoint for all downstream epics. 8 stories, all agent_role=human, all phase:0. status=in-progress; lead=human (Delivery Owner/Operator).
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E1; stories REQ-INFRA-001..008. external_id=E1, external_source=ascp-blueprint. Anchors .kiro REQ-16 (durable storage), REQ-11 (session continuity), REQ-17.4 (sandbox).

modules.name: E2 — Agentic Workspace & Governance Model
modules.description: The Plane workspace governance layer the agents operate inside: the 12-state agent-lifecycle workflow, the 10 custom fields (agent_role, requirement_id, evidence_status, coverage_type, run_state, worktree_branch, agent_run_id, ears_pattern, gate, output_hash), the 22-label taxonomy, and the project identifier/description. Defines who-can-do-what and how state moves. 3 stories (implementer/initializer/human), all phase:0. status=in-progress; lead=human.
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E2 §1 (states/fields/labels). external_id=E2, external_source=ascp-blueprint. This module owns the board's own configuration as first-class work items.

modules.name: E3 — Intent→Spec Compilation & Coverage Model
modules.description: The front of the pipeline: the initializer compiling intent into EARS requirements, the Z3-backed spec_validator (consistency/completeness/vacuity/independence + vague-adjective scanner), the feature_list.schema.json coverage model, omission-declaration emission, and the Research-triggered domain-baseline checklists. Turns raw intent into a verified, fully-mapped coverage model where every item starts unproven. 6 stories (initializer ×4, research ×1, human ×1), mostly phase:0 + 1 phase:2. status=in-progress; lead=initializer-role.
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E3; .kiro tasks 2,4,10.1,10.4,12. Anchors REQ-SPEC-*, REQ-COV-*. external_id=E3, external_source=ascp-blueprint.

modules.name: E4 — Verification Engine & Evidence
modules.description: The independent evaluator and its proof artifacts: the verifier's 5 layers (structural lint/type/AST, semantic unit+integration, behavioral Playwright trace/screenshot, security Semgrep+CodeQL, perf/a11y k6/Lighthouse/axe), the evidence_collector that assembles the four-field Evidence_Record (test_file, test_name, output_hash sha256, collected_at), and the coverage≥85% flip from unproven→proven. The module that produces the proof the completion gate demands. 4 stories (verifier ×3, implementer ×1), phase:0 + phase:1. status=planned→in-progress; lead=verifier-role.
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E4; .kiro tasks 3,8,10.3,15,51. Anchors REQ-VERIFY-*, REQ-EXEC-012 (WIRING integration evidence). external_id=E4, external_source=ascp-blueprint.

modules.name: E5 — Completion & Quality-Gate Automation
modules.description: The fail-closed completion machinery: the Stop hook coverage gate (unproven→exit 2 block; cap/budget/no-progress→HANDOFF exit 0), the OPA coverage_query.rego zero-evidence merge policy, the GitHub ruleset required status checks (formal-verification, coverage-gate, secrets-scan, audit-chain-verify, traceability-gate, zap-baseline, deepeval-gate + human reviewer), and the NFR quality gates (perf/a11y/eval). The module that decides what is allowed to merge. 5 stories (verifier ×4, human ×1), spanning phase:0/1/2/3. status=planned; lead=verifier-role.
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E5; .kiro tasks 5,13,14,21,55,56. Anchors REQ-GATE-*, REQ-COV-002/003, REQ-EVAL-001, REQ-SEC-008. external_id=E5, external_source=ascp-blueprint.

modules.name: E6 — Agent Integration Layer — Plane ⇄ Agent (Bidirectional)
modules.description: The two-way bridge between the agent loop and this Plane board: agent runs create/advance Plane issues through the 12 states, gate decisions push webhooks, the traceability triple (issue ↔ worktree_branch ↔ agent_run_id) is kept in sync, and human actions in Plane (plan approval, HANDOFF pickup) feed back to the agents. The integration module that makes the board a live mirror of the run, not a manual log. 7 stories (human ×3, verifier ×2, implementer ×1, research ×1), spanning phase:0/2/3/4. status=planned; lead=human (orchestrator).
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E6; Plane Webhooks/REST sync + .kiro REQ-INTEG-*. external_id=E6, external_source=ascp-blueprint. Largest cross-phase span — the integration seam.

modules.name: E7 — Observability, Audit & Traceability
modules.description: The proof-of-work and provenance layer: the immutable gate_audit_log + audit-chain-verify, OTel gate-decision telemetry, the orphan_detector + traceability_writer (every commit carries a requirement_id, every requirement has a verification artifact), the requirement↔story↔task↔evidence RTM, and the Plane dashboards/Command Center. The module that answers 'which requirement, which run, what evidence, why this decision' for any item. 6 stories (human ×5, verifier ×1), mostly phase:2/3. status=planned; lead=human (auditor).
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E7; .kiro tasks 20,29,36,52 + REQ-OBS-*, REQ-AUDIT-*, REQ-TRACE-*. external_id=E7, external_source=ascp-blueprint.

modules.name: E8 — Orchestration, Anti-Loopmaxxing & Human-in-the-Loop
modules.description: The control and safety layer: the bounded loop (iteration cap DEFAULT 25, spec-completion 7-pass cap, no-progress N=3 window), the HANDOFF/escalation path (halt-and-escalate must allow the stop — the fixed infinite-block defect), the kill-switch (flagd, disable an agent capability ≤30s without restart), the centralized execution-bounds config, and the human-in-the-loop gates (plan approval, checklist approval, inferred-item confirmation). The module that guarantees the loop can never run away and a human is always in the seat. 10 stories (human ×4, implementer ×2, research ×2, verifier ×2), phase:0 + phase:3. status=in-progress; lead=human.
modules.maps_to: Blueprint Epic E8; .kiro tasks 44,57 + REQ-LOOP-005, REQ-CTRL-001, REQ-STATE-*. external_id=E8, external_source=ascp-blueprint. Owns the anti-loopmaxxing invariants.

views.name: 1 · Pipeline (default board)
views.layout: Board (Kanban) — the only 2-D layout. Publish read-only after building, for zero-context stakeholder onboarding.
views.group_by: State (the 12 agent-lifecycle states, rendered left→right in workflow order because states were created in pipeline sequence): Backlog → Agent-Triaged → Spec-Compiling → Spec-Verified → Plan-Approved → Agent-Executing → In-Verification → Human-Review → Done, then the 3 cancelled terminals HANDOFF / Blocked / Failed.
views.display_props: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Cycle, Module, Sub-work-item Count, Due Date. (Disable Start Date / Estimate / Attachment / Link to keep cards compact.)
views.purpose: The flagship single screen. Sub-group-by Module gives one horizontal swimlane per epic (E1-E8) so a viewer sees, with zero context, exactly where every item sits in the agent lifecycle AND which epic owns it. Toggles: Show empty groups = ON (all 12 state columns always visible, so an empty column is meaningful), Show sub-work-items = ON (story→task nesting visible). Sub-group-by = Module.

views.name: 2 · By-Epic Roadmap
views.layout: Board (Kanban); plus a cloned 'By-Epic — Timeline' (Gantt) companion for the dependency/critical-path read across waves.
views.display_props: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Sub-work-item Count, Cycle.
views.purpose: Inverts View 1 into an epic-first roadmap. Columns = the 8 epics; Sub-group-by = State so each epic shows its distribution across the lifecycle — instantly surfacing epics stuck pre-Spec-Verified vs nearing Done. Order-by = Manual to hand-rank epics by delivery priority. Show empty groups = ON. Timeline clone: Layout=Timeline, Group by=Module, Start/Due dates set so waves render as bars with dependency lines.

views.name: 3 · By-Agent-Role Workload
views.group_by: Labels — filtered to the agent-role label subset only, giving one column per role: agent:initializer, agent:coder (implementer), agent:tester (verifier), agent:research, human:review.
views.filters: Labels ∈ {agent:initializer, agent:coder, agent:tester, agent:research, human:review}. (Equivalently filter on the agent_role field.) Show empty groups = OFF to hide non-role labels.
views.display_props: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Cycle, Module, Due Date.
views.purpose: The agentic analogue of Plane's 'group by State × sub-group by Assignees' workload pattern — substitute role-Labels for Assignees because work is owned by agent ROLE, not a human. Sub-group-by = State so each role's queue shows where it is backing up in the lifecycle. Reveals over/under-loaded roles and verification bottlenecks. Keep role labels mutually exclusive (one role label per item) for a clean read.

views.name: 4 · By-Phase / Sprint
views.layout: List for the dense planning read; clone as Board for the active-sprint standup.
views.filters: None for the full-roadmap variant; add Cycle = current for an 'active phase only' variant.
views.display_props: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Module, Sub-work-item Count, Due Date.
views.purpose: Maps the 7 platform phases (0-6) to the 7 Cycles for a phase-by-phase, sprint-rhythm read. Because Cycles carry start/end dates + burndown, this is the velocity / phase-completion view. Board variant adds Sub-group-by = State for the standup. Order-by = Priority. Show empty groups = ON so future phases stay visible.

views.name: 5 · Blocked & HANDOFF (exception cockpit)
views.layout: Board (Kanban). Publish read-only so stakeholders can watch the blocker queue.
views.filters: State ∈ {HANDOFF, Blocked, Failed} (the 3 off-happy-path cancelled terminals). Optional secondary Priority ∈ {Urgent, High} for a 'critical blockers' variant.
views.display_props: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Module, Cycle, Link, Updated date.
views.purpose: The human operator's exception-handling cockpit — the single place the lifecycle's three escape hatches are visible together: HANDOFF (cap/budget/no-progress → halt-and-escalate), Blocked (a gate returned exit 2 — unproven dep, failed integrity/approval), Failed (verification failed, evidence withheld). Order-by = Last Updated surfaces the stalest blockers first. Show empty groups = ON so an empty board visibly means 'nothing blocked'.

views.name: 6 · Evidence-Pending / Verification Queue
views.layout: Table (spreadsheet) — maximal column density + bulk action for the audit/traceability mandate.
views.filters: State = In-Verification (the gate where agent output awaits proof). AND optionally evidence_status = unproven, or a label like type:* to slice by coverage type.
views.display_props: FULL set — ID, Work-item Type, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Cycle, Modules, Estimate, Start Date, Due Date, Sub-work-item Count, Attachment Count, Link. (The custom fields requirement_id, output_hash, agent_run_id, worktree_branch are the load-bearing audit columns.)
views.purpose: The audit / proof-of-work view. Table layout exposes every property as a column so a reviewer can scan evidence completeness (does each item have a four-field Evidence_Record: test_file, test_name, output_hash, collected_at?) across the whole queue and bulk-advance verified items to Human-Review or Done. Order-by = Last Updated.

views.name: 7 · Current-Sprint Execution Board (WIP-capped)
views.display_props: ID, State, Priority, Assignee, Labels, Module, Sub-work-item Count, Due Date.
views.purpose: The day-to-day execution board for whichever phase is active. Because Plane has no hard per-column WIP gate, the limits are encoded in the VIEW NAME and the onboarding page: 'Execution Board (WIP: Agent-Executing≤5, In-Verification≤3, Human-Review≤2)'. These three columns silently back up in an agentic loop (the agent produces faster than verification/review drains); when a column maxes, the team swarms to clear it before pulling new work. Order-by = Priority. Show empty groups = ON. (Optional 7th view — drop if a 6-view set is preferred; the WIP discipline can also live on View 1.)

onboarding_page_markdown:
# ASCP — Start Here

> **Agentic SDLC Control Plane.** This board is the human-facing surface of an autonomous Spec-to-Evidence delivery engine. Agents turn intent into *verified* software; this board is where every coverage item, gate decision, agent run, and HANDOFF shows up as a work item moving through a twelve-state lifecycle behind a **fail-closed** completion gate.

If you read nothing else: **an item reaches `Done` only when it is *proven* with a complete four-field Evidence_Record and every gate (Stop + OPA + CI) passes.** Nothing merges on a promise.

---

## 1. Mission

ASCP delivers software the way an AI lab would want it delivered: **intent → EARS requirements → coverage model → isolated implementation → independent verification → evidence → merge**, with autonomous agents acting as *memory*, *verifier*, *scope-enforcer*, and *auditor*, and a hard gate that refuses to call anything finished without proof.

The work is the live build of that engine itself: **243 work items** — **8 epics → 49 EARS user stories → 186 tasks** — sequenced across **7 delivery phases** and grounded in the reconciled `.kiro/specs/spec-to-evidence-control` spec (32 EARS requirements · 32 properties · 8 durable tables · 57 tasks · 49 dependency waves).

---

## 2. The 12-state agent lifecycle (legend)

Work items flow left→right through nine happy-path states, then can drop into one of three terminal "cancelled" states. Board columns are in this exact order.

| State | Plane group | What it means |
|---|---|---|
| **Backlog** | backlog | Unrefined intake. Not yet scoped. |
| **Agent-Triaged** | unstarted | Classified by the *initializer*; `agent_role` + `requirement_id` assigned. |
| **Spec-Compiling** | started | Initializer compiling EARS + running `spec_validator` (Z3). |
| **Spec-Verified** | started | `violation_count == 0`; `feature_list.json` emitted (all items **unproven**). |
| **Plan-Approved** | started | Human plan approval written (`plan-approved.json`, SHA-bound). |
| **Agent-Executing** | started | Implementer building **one** slice in an isolated git worktree. |
| **In-Verification** | started | Verifier running the 5-layer checks; capturing the Evidence_Record. |
| **Human-Review** | started | PR open + human reviewer gate. |
| **Done** | completed | **Proven** with evidence; Stop + OPA + CI all pass. |
| **HANDOFF** | cancelled | Cap / budget / no-progress reached → handed to a human. *Distinct from Done.* |
| **Blocked** | cancelled | A gate returned exit 2 (unproven dependency, failed integrity/approval). |
| **Failed** | cancelled | Verification failed; evidence withheld. |

> **Why three "cancelled" terminals?** They are the lifecycle's escape hatches. `HANDOFF` is *halt-and-escalate* (a human takes over — by design it **allows** the run to stop; it does **not** force-continue). `Blocked` is a gate veto. `Failed` is honest failure with no fabricated proof. Live in **View 5** to manage them.

---

## 3. How to read the board

- **Columns = state.** Left of `Done` is in-flight; the three rightmost are exits. All 12 columns always render (empty columns are meaningful — an empty `Blocked` column means nothing is blocked).
- **Swimlanes = epic (Module).** On the Pipeline view, each horizontal lane is one of the 8 epics, so you can see *where* and *who owns it* at once.
- **Cycle = phase.** Each item's Cycle tag is its delivery phase (0-6). Exactly one cycle is *active* at a time — that's the phase currently in flight.
- **Card fields** carry the traceability triple: `requirement_id` (which requirement), `worktree_branch` (which code), `agent_run_id` (which agent run), plus `output_hash` (the evidence). `coverage_type` is functional / NFR / WIRING.
- **Priority** `priority:blocking` (or Urgent) means a dependency is waiting on it — clear these first.

---

## 4. The state machine

```
                 ┌─────────────────────────── happy path ───────────────────────────┐
 Backlog → Agent-Triaged → Spec-Compiling → Spec-Verified → Plan-Approved →
           Agent-Executing → In-Verification → Human-Review → Done ✓

 escape hatches (any in-flight state may exit here):
   · cap / budget / no-progress (N=3) ──────────────▶ HANDOFF   (gate ALLOWS stop)
   · gate veto / exit 2 (unproven dep, bad approval) ▶ Blocked
   · verification fails, evidence withheld ──────────▶ Failed   (→ may re-enter as unproven)
```

**Gate semantics (the contract the whole board rests on):**
- **Stop hook** — any in-scope `unproven` item ⇒ **exit 2 (block, force-continue)**. Cap / budget / no-progress ⇒ write `status=handoff`, **exit 0 (allow stop)**. *Only the unproven case blocks* — this is the fixed infinite-block defect: HANDOFF must let the run end.
- **PreToolUse** — plan-approval, scope-sequencing, artifact-guard, status-transition, schema-validation, checklist-approval guards, evaluated in order, first block wins.
- **SubagentStop** — rejects any Evidence_Record missing one of its four fields (`test_file`, `test_name`, `output_hash`, `collected_at`).
- **OPA + CI** — at merge: zero in-scope item may be non-`proven`, and every proven item must carry complete 4-field evidence. Required checks: `formal-verification`, `coverage-gate`, `secrets-scan`, `audit-chain-verify`, `traceability-gate`, `zap-baseline`, `deepeval-gate` + a human reviewer.

Permitted status edges: `unproven → proven` (requires complete evidence), `unproven → failed`, `failed → unproven`. **Everything else is blocked**, including identity mutations and any into-`proven` without evidence.

---

## 5. Traceability: requirement → story → task → evidence

Every line of work is traceable end-to-end, both directions:

```
 Epic (E1–E8, a Module)
   └─ EARS User Story  (requirement_id, e.g. REQ-INFRA-001 — a "shall" statement)
        └─ Task        (one atomic slice, ≤15 min / ≤1 feature, one worktree)
             └─ Subtask (only where a task genuinely decomposes)
                  └─ Evidence_Record  { test_file, test_name, output_hash: sha256:…, collected_at }
                       └─ Links: PR · CI run · test report · .kiro spec section
```

- **Forward:** every source unit references a `requirement_id`; un-annotated units are `# orphan-exempt`.
- **Backward:** every requirement has a verification artifact (a `traceability_links` row of type test/evidence, or an `evidence_records` row). The `orphan_detector` fails the merge on any orphan in either direction.
- **The traceability triple** — *issue ↔ `worktree_branch` ↔ `agent_run_id`* — ties every Plane item to the exact code and the exact agent run that produced it.

---

## 6. The views (your navigation set)

| # | View | Use it to… |
|---|---|---|
| **1** | **Pipeline** (Board · State × Epic swimlanes) | See, in one screen, where all 243 items sit and which epic owns each. *The default.* |
| **2** | **By-Epic Roadmap** (Board / Timeline · Module) | Read delivery epic-first; spot epics stuck pre-Spec-Verified. |
| **3** | **By-Agent-Role Workload** (Board · role-Label × State) | See which agent role (initializer / coder / tester / research / human) is overloaded or backing up. |
| **4** | **By-Phase / Sprint** (List/Board · Cycle) | Phase-by-phase progress + burndown across phases 0-6. |
| **5** | **Blocked & HANDOFF** (Board · State ∈ {HANDOFF, Blocked, Failed}) | The exception cockpit — the one place every escape hatch is visible. Operators live here. |
| **6** | **Evidence-Pending** (Table · State = In-Verification) | Audit the verification queue; scan four-field evidence completeness; bulk-advance. |
| **7** | **Current-Sprint Execution** (Board · Cycle = current, WIP-capped) | Day-to-day execution of the active phase. **WIP caps: Agent-Executing ≤5, In-Verification ≤3, Human-Review ≤2** — swarm a maxed column before pulling new work. |

---

## 7. The 8 epics (feature map) & 7 phases (time map)

**Epics (Modules — *what*):**
`E1` Core Plane Infrastructure & Self-Hosting · `E2` Agentic Workspace & Governance Model · `E3` Intent→Spec Compilation & Coverage Model · `E4` Verification Engine & Evidence · `E5` Completion & Quality-Gate Automation · `E6` Agent Integration Layer (Plane ⇄ Agent) · `E7` Observability, Audit & Traceability · `E8` Orchestration, Anti-Loopmaxxing & Human-in-the-Loop.

**Phases (Cycles — *when*):**
`0` Spine (the hard prerequisite — all spine components coded + smoke-tested first) · `1` Verification Depth · `2` Durable State · `3` Observability · `4` Property-Based Tests (full suite) · `5` Durable Orchestration *(optional)* · `6` Predictive Routing *(optional)*.

Epics and phases are **orthogonal**: an item belongs to one epic (Module) *and* one phase (Cycle) at once — e.g. a verifier task in **E4** that ships in **Phase 1** carries Module=E4 **and** Cycle=Phase 1.

---

## 8. Conventions at a glance

- **IDs:** `ASCP-1` … `ASCP-243`. Requirements use `REQ-<DOMAIN>-NNN` (e.g. `REQ-GATE-002`).
- **One role label per item.** Keep `agent:*` / `human:review` mutually exclusive so View 3 reads cleanly.
- **Nothing lingers in `Backlog`** unscoped. **Close epics + archive Done/Failed** at each phase boundary so burndown stays honest.
- **WIP is a policy, not a wall** — encoded in View 7's name and here. When `In-Verification` or `Human-Review` maxes, the agent loop is outrunning verification: swarm, don't pull.

*Owner of this page: the Delivery Owner / Operator. This page is Locked — edit via the owner. Last reconciled against the `.kiro` spec on board build.*
conventions: IDENTIFIER & IDs — Project identifier = ASCP (≤12 uppercase chars, no specials; hyphen/period blocked), yielding work items ASCP-1 … ASCP-243. Keep the project description to one sentence ('Plane control plane for the autonomous Spec-to-Evidence delivery engine: intent→verified software, fail-closed'); the full narrative lives on the Start Here page, not the description field.

NAMING — Epics named by OUTCOME not activity (E4 'Verification Engine & Evidence', not 'Build verifier'). Stories = EARS shall-statements keyed REQ-<DOMAIN>-NNN — keep them as precise testable 'WHEN <trigger> the system SHALL <response>' lines, NOT narrative user stories (EARS rigor is the opposite of bloat). Tasks = short imperative titles ('Add wave-dependency resolver'), minimal description, link the .kiro spec section rather than restating it.

TWO ORTHOGONAL AXES — Phase = Cycle (WHEN, the time axis: 7 sequential phase-cycles, exactly one active); Epic = Module (WHAT, the feature axis: 8 modules E1-E8). An item lives in exactly ONE cycle but its module is independent — assign every item to both its phase-cycle and its epic-module. Never map waves to cycles (49 cycles is unusable and waves are a dependency DAG, not a calendar) — waves stay as the wave-NN label / custom property.

STATE ORDERING — Create the 12 states in pipeline sequence (Backlog → Agent-Triaged → Spec-Compiling → Spec-Verified → Plan-Approved → Agent-Executing → In-Verification → Human-Review → Done, then HANDOFF/Blocked/Failed) with the correct Plane groups (backlog / unstarted / started×6 / completed / cancelled×3), because Kanban column order follows state-definition order — there is no per-view state re-ordering.

LABEL COMPACTION — Keep the 22 labels prefixed and orthogonal so they self-group in the picker: agent:* (initializer/coder/tester/research) + human:review for ROLE; priority:* (blocking/high/normal); type:* (functional/nfr/wiring); gate:* (stop/completion/security); phase:0-4; and the 3 status mirrors handoff/blocked/failed. Audit rule: never create a label that restates a State, a Module(epic), a Cycle(phase) or the Priority field — those dimensions are structured fields, not labels. One role label per item (mutually exclusive) so View 3 stays clean.

WIP DISCIPLINE — Plane has no hard per-column WIP gate, so encode caps in the view NAME and the onboarding page: Agent-Executing ≤5, In-Verification ≤3, Human-Review ≤2 (the three columns that silently back up when the agent loop outruns verification). When a column maxes, swarm to clear it before pulling new work. Re-derive caps from cycle burndown after ~2 phases of observed occupancy.

HIERARCHY — Strict 4 levels only: Epic → Story → Task → Subtask. Apply the single-child rule: if a level would contain only one child, flatten it (a one-child epic is mislabeled). Epics via Work Item Types (enable in Project Settings → Features); children via the Parent field; the deepest leaf is a sub-work-item. Module is a flat grouping ON TOP of this tree, not a replacement for it.

IDEMPOTENT PROVISIONING — Stamp external_id (e.g. E3, REQ-INFRA-001, phase-0) + external_source ('ascp-blueprint' / 'kiro-spec') on every cycle, module, and work item created via REST so re-runs reconcile (GET … match on external_id → PATCH) instead of duplicating. Keep a kiro-key → plane-id map in the provisioning state file.

BACKLOG HYGIENE — A short recurring triage each phase boundary beats sporadic sweeps: nothing lingers in Backlog unscoped (Backlog → Agent-Triaged is the intake funnel); archive each cycle's Done/Failed items and close completed epics the moment they finish so burndown and phase rollups stay accurate; carry incomplete items forward only via the transfer-issues API (snapshots progress) — never by hand-editing dates.

API REALITY — Cycles, Modules, work items, states, labels, custom properties, property values, relations, links, and Pages-on-cloud are REST-driveable and should be provisioned by script; SAVED VIEWS and DASHBOARDS (and Pages on self-hosted, issues #8986/#7319/#8598) are WEB-UI ONLY — build the 6-7 views, the dashboards, and the Start Here page by hand ONCE and capture them in this board-navigation guide. Respect the 60 req/min rate limit (batch cycle-issues/module-issues as one array call per cycle/module; back off on X-RateLimit-Remaining).
traceability_notes:
PROVISIONING SOURCE OF TRUTH. The board is reconciled from two on-disk artifacts in this repo: docs/plane/plane_backlog.json (8 epics, 49 stories, 186 tasks, the 12-state/22-label/10-field vocab) and docs/plane/PLANE_BLUEPRINT.md (the full EARS-story + acceptance-criteria narrative). The phase taxonomy comes from .kiro/specs/spec-to-evidence-control/tasks.md (### Phase 0-4 headings + the overview line declaring Phase 5/6 optional). Drive provisioning from these; do not hand-author.

WHAT MAPS TO WHAT (decisive, from the research's one-cycle-per-item constraint).
- 7 platform PHASES (0-6) → 7 CYCLES (sequential, exactly one active). NOT the 49 waves — 49 cycles is unusable and waves are a dependency ordering, not a calendar. Waves stay as a `wave-NN` label / custom DECIMAL property so wave membership is filterable WITHOUT consuming the single cycle slot.
- 8 EPICS (E1-E8) → 8 MODULES (orthogonal feature axis; an item is in one Module AND one Cycle simultaneously). Stamp external_id=E1..E8.
- 49 EARS STORIES → Story-typed work items (parent = their epic). 186 TASKS → Task-typed (parent = their story). Subtasks only where a task genuinely decomposes (single-child rule).
- 12 custom STATES + 22 LABELS + 10 custom FIELDS are provisioned exactly as the blueprint §1.1-1.3 enumerates.

TRACEABILITY SPINE (REST-driveable, build it).
- Custom relations: Story --Implements--> Epic; Task --Verifies/Tests--> Story; verification item --Validates--> Plan (custom relation types created in Workspace Settings, web-UI).
- Custom properties as first-class FILTERABLE fields (not free text): requirement_id (TEXT, the REQ-<DOMAIN>-NNN), coverage_type (OPTION functional/NFR/WIRING), ears_pattern (OPTION), worktree_branch + agent_run_id + output_hash (TEXT) — these three are the traceability triple binding issue↔code↔run. Add Phase (OPTION 0-6) and Wave (DECIMAL) so both axes are queryable even outside Cycle/Module.
- Evidence: attach PR / CI-run / test-report / .kiro-spec URLs via the Work Item Links API; the four-field Evidence_Record (test_file, test_name, output_hash sha256:<64hex>, collected_at) is the proof a status reaches `proven`. The immutable Work Item Activity feed + the gate_audit_log are the audit trail (REQ-AUDIT-001, EU-AI-Act Art.14-style provenance).
- Backward/forward orphan check (orphan_detector, task 20) and commit-has-req-id gate (traceability_writer, task 29) are the CI enforcement that the RTM is complete; they block the merge via the required traceability-gate check.

CROSS-PHASE EPICS ARE EXPECTED, NOT A SMELL. Per the backlog distribution, epics span multiple phases (E5 spans phase 0/1/2/3; E6 spans 0/2/3/4; E7 mostly 2/3). That is exactly why Epic=Module (persistent feature container) and Phase=Cycle (transient timebox) must be separate axes — a single epic's stories are correctly spread across several cycles, and the dual rollup (module completion % vs cycle burndown) is the intended two-axis observability.

GAPS / WEB-UI-ONLY (cannot be scripted — do once by hand, capture here).
- Saved Views (all 6-7) and Dashboards have NO public REST API → build manually in the UI.
- Pages have NO working self-hosted API (issues #8986/#7319/#8598) → author the Start Here page in the web UI, then Lock + optionally Publish read-only.
- Phase 5/6 carry no phase:5/phase:6 label in the current backlog → provision those two cycles EMPTY and assign items via cycle-issues only if/when the optional tracks are greenlit; they must never block the required 0-4 GA.
- WIP caps are not enforced by Plane → policy only, encoded in View 7's name + the onboarding page.

REFERENCES TO ENRICH NEXT. The live Plane instance (http://localhost:8090, workspace ascp, project ASCP, v1.3.1) already exists per task #11; reconcile the provisioned object IDs (state UUIDs, module IDs, cycle IDs, label IDs) back into a kiro-key→plane-id map file before the next provisioning re-run so external_id matching is exact. The blueprint cites design.md line anchors (e.g. design.md:558 eight-table store, :1374 Note N-25 fail-closed) — pull those into the per-epic module descriptions if deeper acceptance-criteria detail is needed.
